name: T1 · Momentum / Rumor Entry
description: Enter on two or more consecutive limit-ups with elevated turnover. Exit when the 5-day average turnover exceeds 20% (sustained flush signal) or after 4 bars, whichever comes first.
rationale: Two consecutive limit-ups indicates strong, sustained demand pressure — institutional accumulation or speculative momentum. The 5-day average turnover exit waits for supply to overwhelm demand rather than reacting to a single spike, aiming to stay in the move longer. Run this against your GCL case-study symbol to see whether the rule generalises.

name: T1-exit-test · Sell One Day Earlier
description: Identical entry to T1 but exits on the first single day that turnover exceeds 30%, rather than waiting for a sustained 5-day average. Tests whether the earlier exit recovers or costs performance.
rationale: GCL reflection: did holding through the turnover peak cost more than it gained? Compare P&L and drawdown against T1 directly. If returns are similar but max drawdown is lower with the earlier exit, that is the method insight. A meaningful difference here changes how you set exits on momentum trades.

name: T2 · Macro-Thesis Entry with Time-Stop
description: Enter when volume surges to 3× the 20-day average — early sign of institutional positioning. Exit on any sharp negative open gap (gap_open_pct < -3%) or after 5 bars, whichever fires first.
rationale: North Copper setup: you saw the macro thesis and entered on volume confirmation of institutional interest. The gap-down exit catches overnight rejection of the move before the day session compounds the loss. The 5-day hard stop prevents thesis overstay when the catalyst fades quietly. Replace universe with your North Copper symbol.

name: T3 · Stealth-Accumulation Screen
description: Screen for stocks with recent dormancy (low 5-day average turnover) that break out on a limit-up with abnormal volume and a compressed price range. Surfaces candidates for manual review — not a buy signal.
rationale: Yabo pattern: institutional accumulation leaves a footprint on a previously quiet stock — a sudden limit-up on volume 3× normal, with a range that was coiling before the move. The low turnover_rate_5d filter screens out stocks that were already active. Each result needs chart, float, and news review before acting.

name: T3-exit-test · False-Breakdown Filter
description: Stealth-accumulation entry (same as T3) but exits only when a sharp down-open is confirmed by above-average volume. Low-volume down-opens are treated as shakeouts and ignored.
rationale: Yabo reflection: a low-volume down-open triggered an early exit that left 25% on the table. This template tests whether requiring volume confirmation (volume_vs_avg20 > 1.5) on the exit signal recovers that missed upside without meaningfully increasing max drawdown. Compare directly against a bare gap_open_pct < -3 exit to measure the difference.
